content.problemStatement:
Chef has an integer array AAA of length NNN such that 1‚Č§Ai‚Č§31 \le A_i \le 31‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§3.

Chef is wondering how many pairs exist in his array, which have a prime sum. Formally, find the number of pairs (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that


1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N
Ai+AjA_i + A_jAi‚Äč+Aj‚Äč is prime.
XXX is a prime integer if it is not 111, and has no other divisor except 111 and XXX. For example, 222, 131313 and 979797 are prime integers, while 111 and 444 are not.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line the number of pairs with prime sums.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : The only pair is (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) which has a sum of 222.
Test Case 2 : The valid pairs are (1,2)(1, 2)(1,2) and (1,3)(1, 3)(1,3), having sums of 333 and 555 respectively.
